paper_title: Going beyond borders after a pandemic: Preparedness of teachers to embrace E-learning in a small Philippine university

entities.description: The primary field of research focusing on higher education and digital transformation.
Author and corresponding researcher from Quirino State University.
Author and researcher from Quirino State University.
A state university in the Philippines where the research was conducted.
The preparedness, skills, and dedication of educators to adopt online platforms for teaching.
The methods and practices used for effective instruction in a virtual environment.
Research design combining quantitative surveys and qualitative interviews.
A quantitative tool used to assess readiness factors like device ownership and internet access.
A qualitative tool used to gather in-depth insights into attitudes and technological capabilities.
A procedure used to ensure the consistency of the research instrument.
Quantitative data collected from 119 higher education teachers.
Qualitative data collected from 10 selected instructors for thematic analysis.
Percentage of instructors who personally own a smartphone.
Percentage of instructors who possess a personal laptop.
Percentage of respondents using cellular data for internet access.
Internal consistency value for the technological skills section of the questionnaire.
Internal consistency value for the attitudes section of the questionnaire.
Internal consistency value for the time management and commitment section.
Mean score representing the instructors' level of commitment and time management.

relations.source: Going beyond borders after a pandemic: Preparedness of teachers to embrace E-learning in a small Philippine university
